_doc: Executable acceptance criteria per spec-20260518-225715 §5.4. Each entry has id, type, given/when/then, check{...}, and ac_uid (sha256(type+given+when+then+JSON.stringify(check))[:16]). Iter-2 revisions: AC2 expanded for B3 (three symlinks + finder + sys.prefix); AC3 re-anchored to structural region locators per S1/codex iter-2 MAJOR #5 + same-JSON-object check per B4/codex iter-2 MAJOR #4 + guard_enforcement_clause per B2/codex iter-2 MAJOR #6; AC5 added for B1 cycle-diff force-include + fail-closed per codex iter-2 BLOCKER #1/#2/#3.

criteria.title: QA empty-active vacuity guard rejects smoking-gun fixture at runtime (manifest mode)
criteria.given: scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py exists in manifest mode (no CLI flags); agents/qa.md Phase 5 references it before report emission; the smoking-gun fixture {active_tests_count:0, pytest_collected_ok:true, pytest_failures:[]} is available on stdin
criteria.when: the verifier executes `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py` (no CLI flags — manifest mode) with each fixture piped on stdin
criteria.then: the script exits 2 with JSON diagnostic on stderr containing verdict:vacuous_rejected for the smoking-gun fixture; exits 0 with verdict:ok for a properly populated non-vacuous fixture; exits 0 with verdict:ok_vacuous_acknowledged when the vacuous shape is explicitly declared

check.mode_note: manifest mode — no CLI flags; stdin-only; pure (codex iter-2 BLOCKER #1)

criteria.title: venv repair script makes ALL THREE symlinks pytest-runnable, eliminates broken symlinks, verifies interpreter is in venv, and is idempotent
criteria.given: scripts/repair-venv.sh exists; /root/.claude/venv/bin/python3 is currently missing (broken-symlink target); pyvenv.cfg declares executable=/usr/bin/python3.12 which exists; pytest is installed in site-packages; user said 'venv 全 broken symlink' (plural), so all three of python, python3, python3.12 must be verified post-repair (QA iter-1 blocker B3)
criteria.when: the verifier executes `bash scripts/repair-venv.sh` once, then runs all three pytest --version invocations + the broken-symlink finder + the sys.prefix check; then executes `bash scripts/repair-venv.sh` a second time and captures the mtime of venv/bin/python3 before vs after
criteria.then: first script run exits 0; ALL THREE of venv/bin/{python,python3,python3.12} -m pytest --version exit 0 with output matching ^pytest\s+\d+\.\d+; `find venv/bin -maxdepth 1 -name 'python*' -type l -xtype l` returns empty; `venv/bin/python -c 'import sys; assert sys.prefix.endswith("venv"), sys.prefix'` exits 0; second script run exits 0 with stdout containing 'already healthy' and venv/bin/python3 mtime unchanged; the script resolves the venv path from its own script location and parses pyvenv.cfg for the executable= line (NOT a hardcoded /usr/bin/python3.12)

steps.name: pytest_runs_python
steps._blocker_ref: QA iter-1 blocker B3 — venv 全 broken symlink (plural); test all three symlinks

steps.name: no_broken_symlinks_remaining
steps._blocker_ref: QA iter-1 blocker B3 — `-xtype l` matches symlinks whose target does not exist; empty output means no broken python* symlinks remain

steps.name: interpreter_lives_in_venv
steps._advisory_ref: QA iter-1 advisory S2 — confirms interpreter genuinely lives in venv, not on system Python with global pytest leaking in

steps.name: no_mutation_on_idempotent
steps.command: stat --printf '%Y' /root/.claude/venv/bin/python3 (compare before/after second run; must be equal)

steps.name: no_hardcoded_target
steps._note: any literal /usr/bin/python3.NN path outside of comments and outside of a pyvenv.cfg parsing context is a hardcoded interpreter target -> fail (codex iter-1 finding #9)

criteria.title: agents/qa.md carries vacuity invariant in BOTH structural regions, replaces smoking-gun example, and binds guard exit code to qa.status
criteria.given: agents/qa.md edits in M1, M3, and M4 are applied
criteria.when: the verifier searches agents/qa.md using STRUCTURAL anchors (Phase 5 region: first `^#### Phase 5: Manifest Verification` line through first subsequent `^#{1,4}\s+` line, NOT including `#####`; manifest_verification JSON region: brace-counted from `"manifest_verification": {` to its matching `}`) — NOT fixed line ranges (QA iter-1 advisory S1; codex iter-2 MAJOR #5)
criteria.then: the literal token vacuous_due_to_empty_active_set appears at least once in each region; the manifest_verification JSON region does NOT simultaneously contain {active_tests_count:0} AND {pytest_collected_ok:true} (same-JSON-object structural check, codex iter-2 MAJOR #4); Phase 5 procedural region explicitly forbids pytest_collected_ok:true when active_tests_count==0; Phase 5 procedural region references scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py; Phase 5 procedural region carries the guard enforcement clause binding exit code 2 → qa.status=fail + primary_cause=qa_oversight + qa.failures[] append (QA iter-1 blocker B2; codex iter-2 MAJOR #6)

phase_5_procedural._doc: Structural anchor: lines from first ^#### Phase 5: Manifest Verification (inclusive) through first subsequent ^#{1,4}\s+ (exclusive — next heading at level <=4; does NOT match ##### sub-subheadings). Replaces iter-1 fragile line range 1070-1120. (QA iter-1 advisory S1; codex iter-2 MAJOR #5.)

manifest_verification_schema._doc: Structural anchor: content from first line matching "manifest_verification": { through the brace-matched closing }. Replaces iter-1 fragile line range 1500-1560. (QA iter-1 advisory S1.)

assertions.name: token_in_procedural_region
assertions._blocker_ref: QA iter-1 advisory S1; codex iter-2 MAJOR #5

assertions.name: guard_enforcement_clause
assertions._blocker_ref: QA iter-1 blocker B2; codex iter-2 MAJOR #6 — concrete report location

sub_assertions.name: qa_failures_append_within_proximity
sub_assertions._doc: Within 600 chars of 'exit code 2', text references qa.failures (append/[]/etc) — provides the concrete structural anchor in the report per codex iter-2 MAJOR #6

assertions.name: no_smoking_gun_example
assertions._blocker_ref: QA iter-1 blocker B4; codex iter-2 MAJOR #4 — same-JSON-object scope check via brace-counting (replaces fragile 5-line proximity)
assertions.predicate: NOT (region contains key-value '"active_tests_count": 0' AND region contains key-value '"pytest_collected_ok": true')
assertions._doc: The schema docblock's worked example MUST be REPLACED (not appended). Per M3, the replacement is the explicit-vacuous shape with active_tests_count:0 + pytest_collected_ok:null + vacuous_due_to_empty_active_set:true. The structural check forbids the (0, true) co-occurrence in the same JSON object regardless of formatting.

criteria.title: guard manifest-mode rejects vacuous reports of every observed shape (regression matrix)
criteria.given: scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py implements the M1 invariant in manifest mode (no CLI flags)
criteria.when: the verifier feeds the script each shape in the regression matrix and captures exit + verdict
criteria.then: every vacuous shape produces exit 2 + verdict:vacuous_rejected

criteria.title: guard cycle-diff mode computes active_tests_count from per-file pytest-collectable subset; fail-closed on environment failure
criteria.given: scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py supports cycle-diff mode via --cycle-diff-files <comma-paths> + --collect-only-cmd '<cmd>'; a synthetic cycle-diff fixture is prepared containing exactly two paths: (a) tests/generated/<tmp>/test_force_include_synthetic.py with body literally `def test_x(): assert True` (one pytest-collectable test) and (b) scripts/some_module.py with body irrelevant (one non-test python module)
criteria.when: the verifier runs the guard with cycle-diff flags against the synthetic fixture, then against an empty/no-.py diff, then with one flag missing, then with a bogus collect-only command
criteria.then: the synthetic test+module diff produces exit 0 + verdict:ok + active_tests_count:1 (only the collectable test counted, not the module); the empty/no-.py diff produces exit 0 + verdict:ok_vacuous_acknowledged + vacuous_reason containing 'no pytest-collectable files in cycle diff'; one missing flag produces exit 3 + verdict:guard_blocked; bogus collect-only command produces exit 3 + verdict:guard_blocked (NEVER falls back to file-name heuristics) (QA iter-1 blocker B1; codex iter-2 BLOCKER #1/#2/#3)

_setup._doc: Before running steps, the verifier creates tests/generated/<tmp>/test_force_include_synthetic.py (one collectable test) and scripts/some_module.py (one non-test .py file); they are removed in teardown. <tmp> is the cycle's temp dir.

steps.name: force_include_one_collectable
steps._blocker_ref: QA iter-1 blocker B1; codex iter-2 BLOCKER #3 — per-file collection: each .py file is individually fed to collect-only; only files with >=1 collected test are counted

steps.name: empty_diff_vacuous_acknowledged
steps.expected_vacuous_reason_contains: no pytest-collectable files in cycle diff
steps._blocker_ref: QA iter-1 blocker B1 — empty cycle diff must emit ok_vacuous_acknowledged with the specific vacuous_reason string

steps.name: no_py_files_vacuous_acknowledged
steps.expected_vacuous_reason_contains: no pytest-collectable files in cycle diff

steps.name: missing_collect_only_cmd_flag
steps._blocker_ref: Codex iter-2 BLOCKER #2 — one of the two cycle-diff flags missing is a CLI usage error treated as guard_blocked (no partial-mode degradation)

steps.name: bogus_collect_only_command_fail_closed
steps._blocker_ref: Codex iter-2 BLOCKER #2 — pytest unavailable MUST fail closed with verdict:guard_blocked; NEVER fall back to file-name heuristics like test_*.py (would recreate the grep-only degradation this cycle exists to kill)
